spec_match.rationale: The probe iframe is loaded from a `blob:` URL whose network isolation key changes per run, and the script body uses `eval()` — both of which the feature's own sibling concept (`size-threshold-explorer`) lists as cache-disqualifying. So the second run is never actually a warm cache hit; the page measures nothing about the new feature.

interactive.rationale: Each click of `Run a load` instantiates an iframe, measures real timings via postMessage, and appends a row to a live results table — a real interactive surface.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises a `PerformanceObserver` compile-mark readout; the page only measures wall-clock time between iframe `load` and last-statement execution, with the column labelled `script compile-mark` actually showing total elapsed time.

edge_cases.rationale: No edge cases at all — no demonstration of what happens when the script changes by one byte (should be a cold miss again), when the iframe is cross-origin (different NIK key), or when `eval()` disqualifies caching.

design_system.rationale: Uses design tokens, the rose Reset button override sets background AND color together, no static-card.

openQuestions.title: Load the inline-script probe from a same-origin URL, not a blob
openQuestions.detail: Blob URLs get a fresh network-isolation key per run, so V8's content-hash cache cannot hit. Either add a server route under `v149/inline-script-cache/cold-vs-warm/probe` that serves the same HTML body every time, or write the iframe content to a worker-cached endpoint. Without this the headline measurement is meaningless.

openQuestions.title: Drop `eval()` from the probe script
openQuestions.detail: The cache is content-hash keyed, so any script that disqualifies itself (eval, new Function, with, document.write) never enters the cache. Replace the `eval('f'+i)(i)` call with a direct dispatch table — otherwise the demo is benchmarking the un-cacheable path.

openQuestions.title: Surface compile-marks via PerformanceObserver
openQuestions.detail: The blurb on the feature index promises `PerformanceObserver compile-marks`. Wire up an observer for `mark`/`measure` entries with the prefix `script-` (or v8 perf names) so the readout actually shows compile time, not total iframe lifetime.

openQuestions.title: Add a single-byte-change run to show miss-on-modify
openQuestions.detail: Once the cache works, the most instructive edge case is `change one character → cold again`. The sibling `cache-key-probe` demo does this for hand-typed content; this page should at least have a `mutate script` button that flips one literal and shows the next run reverting to cold.

summary: The interactive surface and reporting table are well built, but the actual experiment is broken: blob-URL iframes with `eval()`-containing scripts cannot exercise V8's inline-script cache at all, so the cold-vs-warm framing is unsupported. This needs a same-origin probe route and a cache-eligible script body before any of the numbers mean anything.
